headline: The Inflation Debate That Will Shape the Fed’s Plans for Rates
summary: In this episode of WSJ’s Take On the Week, we are joined by Rob Kaplan, vice chairman at Goldman Sachs and a former Federal Reserve president.   We discuss the market reaction to the Supreme Court’s decision to strike down the Trump administration's sweeping tariffs.

headline: Walmart’s Spark Delivery Program Under Fire in $100M FTC Settlement Over Pay, Tips
summary: The commission and 11 states said the retail giant showed inflated base pay estimates via the Spark Driver app and failed to pass through full tips in some deliveries.

headline: Amazon’s Extreme AI Spending Sends Stock to Worst Month in Years
summary: Shares of the e-commerce and cloud computing giant plunged 12% in February, their worst month since December 2022, as Wall Street takes an increasingly jaundiced view of the company’s aggressive AI spending plans.  Not only are the capital expenditures eating into Amazon’s free cash flow, but market pros are growing impatient about when they’ll pay off in dramatic fashion.  The stock also was the worst performer of the so-called Magnificent Seven technology behemoths last month and among the 40 weakest companies in the S&P 500.

content.title: Amazon’s Extreme AI Spending Sends Stock to Worst Month in Years
content.summary: Shares of the e-commerce and cloud computing giant plunged 12% in February, their worst month since December 2022, as Wall Street takes an increasingly jaundiced view of the company’s aggressive AI spending plans.  Not only are the capital expenditures eating into Amazon’s free cash flow, but market pros are growing impatient about when they’ll pay off in dramatic fashion.  The stock also was the worst performer of the so-called Magnificent Seven technology behemoths last month and among the 40 weakest companies in the S&P 500.

headline: The Great Divide: Is Walmart the Ultimate Hedge for 2026?
summary: Walmart (NYSE: WMT) has quietly become one of the more compelling macro stories of 2026. Bank of America recently reinstated coverage with a Buy rating and a $150 price target, arguing the retailer is uniquely built to win in a bifurcated economy where higher earners keep spending and lower earners pull back hard. With shares ... The Great Divide: Is Walmart the Ultimate Hedge for 2026?

headline: Is Walmart (WMT) Still Attractive After 32% One Year Share Price Rally?
summary: If you have ever wondered whether Walmart's share price still offers value after a long run in retail, this breakdown is designed to help you frame that question clearly. Walmart's stock last closed at US$127.95, with returns of 1.7% over 7 days, 7.4% over 30 days, 13.5% year to date, 32.4% over 1 year and 183.1% over 3 years. This naturally raises questions about how much of the story is already reflected in the price. Alongside these returns, investors have been weighing ongoing headlines...
